businessSummary.oneLiner: Leading AI data analytics platform for government and enterprise — Gotham (defense), Foundry (enterprise), AIP (AI platform) trifecta
businessSummary.description: Palantir provides data integration, analytics, and AI decision-making platforms for government and commercial organizations. FY2025 revenue $4.48B (+70% YoY), adjusted operating margin 57%. Thursday close $153.50 (+1.25%). Defied the broader market selloff (S&P -1.52%) driven by AIPCon 9 launch + Karp's comments on AI elevating US wartime capabilities. CEO Karp confirmed Claude still in use despite Pentagon blacklist. Michael Burry criticized 'no real AI of its own.' Market cap $368.1B, P/E ~244x, Forward P/E ~116x.
howTheyMakeMoney: Government contracts: Gotham/Apollo — defense, intelligence, NATO (~55% revenue)
Commercial contracts: Foundry/AIP — enterprise AI platform, Boot Camp strategy (~45% revenue)
SaaS model: Annual subscriptions + expansion. Net Dollar Retention 120%+

keyMetrics.name: P/E (TTM)
keyMetrics.interpretation: Based on EPS $0.63. Highest premium in AI sector

keyMetrics.name: Forward P/E
keyMetrics.interpretation: FY2026E EPS $1.32 basis. High but reflects growth

keyMetrics.name: Revenue Growth (YoY)
keyMetrics.interpretation: FY2025. Accelerating on AI demand explosion

keyMetrics.name: ROE
keyMetrics.interpretation: High return on equity. Capital-efficient growth

growthDrivers.title: AIPCon 9 — Counter-Rally Catalyst Amid Market Selloff
growthDrivers.description: 3/12: AIPCon 9 launched with new customer case studies + CEO Karp telling CNBC that 'AI is elevating US wartime capabilities.' PLTR rose +1.25% while S&P fell -1.52%.

growthDrivers.title: Forbes: 'Is Palantir the New Leader of the AI Revolution?'
growthDrivers.description: 3/10: +84% one-year return outperforming most competitors. Structural leadership in AI data analytics reaffirmed.

growthDrivers.title: Seeking Alpha: 'The Rebound Is Just Beginning' — Strong Buy
growthDrivers.description: 3/9: Government + commercial segments both accelerating. Surging defense demand and AI adoption mark the start of a rebound.

growthDrivers.title: Pentagon Claude Blacklist → Alternative AI Supplier Opportunity
growthDrivers.description: Karp: 'DoW planning to phase out Anthropic but not yet phased out.' Alternative AI model integration could further enhance Palantir's platform value.

competitiveAdvantage.summary: 20+ years in government/defense AI with security clearance barriers. 'Ontology' platform as the unique moat for AI-human collaboration interface.

moats.description: Top-secret clearance with US DoD and intelligence agencies. Impenetrable barrier for competitors.
AI and human agents' 'tool factory.' Unmatched in battlefield planning and target identification.
5-day intensive pilots convert to enterprise-wide deployments. High conversion rates.

competitors.name: Microsoft (Azure AI)
competitors.detail: Cloud AI platform. General-purpose vs Palantir's specialized analytics

competitors.name: Snowflake
competitors.detail: Data lakehouse. Partial overlap in analytics domain

competitors.name: C3.ai
competitors.detail: Enterprise AI competitor. Inferior in scale and profitability

competitors.name: Anduril
competitors.detail: Defense AI startup. Rapidly growing but smaller than Palantir

recentNews.headline: PLTR +1.25% — AIPCon 9 counter-rally amid market crash
recentNews.significance: Rose +1.25% while S&P fell -1.52% and Nasdaq -1.78%. AIPCon 9 new customer reveals + CEO Karp wartime AI interview drove outperformance.

recentNews.headline: Karp: 'Still using Claude' despite Pentagon blacklist
recentNews.significance: CNBC: DoD designated Anthropic as 'supply-chain risk' but Palantir systems still integrated. AI model transition is the challenge.

recentNews.headline: Michael Burry: 'Palantir has no real AI software of its own'
recentNews.significance: Benzinga: 'Big Short' investor Burry critiques technical dependency and ethical failures, citing tragic military strike. Maintains short position.

recentNews.headline: TheStreet Pro: Rating maintained ahead of AIPCon 9 — Resistance at $163, $168
recentNews.significance: Short-term upside expected from AIPCon but $163/$168 technical resistance. Take-profit warning.

recentNews.headline: Forbes: 'Is Palantir the New Leader of the AI Revolution?'
recentNews.significance: +84% one-year return outperforming most competitors. Structural AI analytics leadership reaffirmed.

analystOpinions.notableComment: 16 of 28 analysts rate Buy. Post-AIPCon 9 target upgrades possible. Seeking Alpha maintains Strong Buy. Michael Burry's short vs Wall Street bulls — a battlefield of opinions.

risks.title: Extreme Valuation — P/E ~244x
risks.description: Forward P/E ~116x is highest in AI sector. Growth deceleration would cause rapid premium compression.

risks.title: Michael Burry 'No Real AI' Critique + Ethical Risk
risks.description: 'Big Short' investor publicly criticizing technical dependency and military AI ethics. Maintaining short position.

risks.title: Pentagon Claude Blacklist — AI Supplier Transition Risk
risks.description: DoD phasing out Anthropic. Palantir must integrate alternative AI models. Transition costs and performance risks.

risks.title: Insider Selling Continues
risks.description: CEO Karp + Director Thiel continue large-scale sales. Partially offset by rising institutional ownership.

risks.title: Iran Conflict Resolution → Defense Premium Reduction
risks.description: War ending could reduce defense AI premium, though structural digitization trend persists.

buyReasons.title: AIPCon 9 Counter-Rally Shows Narrative Strength
buyReasons.rationale: +1.25% amid S&P -1.52% crash. CEO Karp's wartime AI comments + new customers sustain momentum.

buyReasons.title: Revenue +70% YoY + FCF Margin 47% — Growth and Profitability
buyReasons.rationale: SaaS leverage generating $4.4B revenue with minimal infrastructure. Net cash $6.95B.

buyReasons.title: Seeking Alpha: 'Rebound Just Beginning' — Government + Commercial Dual Acceleration
buyReasons.rationale: Defense AI demand surge + AIP Boot Camp commercial customer acquisition. Early-stage rebound.

buyReasons.title: Institutional Ownership 7%→32% — Structural Demand Shift
buyReasons.rationale: Retail-to-institutional transition increasing price stability and upside potential.

overallOpinion: Palantir closed Thursday at $153.50 (+1.25%), one of the few stocks to rise as the S&P 500 hit its 2026 closing low.

AIPCon 9 provided the catalyst — CEO Karp told CNBC that 'AI is elevating US wartime capabilities' and confirmed Claude remains in use despite the Pentagon blacklist. Meanwhile, Michael Burry doubled down on his 'no real AI software' critique, maintaining his short position.

The central question: is a P/E of ~244x justified? Revenue +70% YoY, FCF margin 47%, institutional ownership at 32% — growth metrics are overwhelming. Technically holding above the 50-day SMA ($147.50) with $163/$168 resistance as the next hurdle. Burry's short vs Wall Street's bullishness creates a battlefield.
